evals.prompt: Compare Hugo, Gatsby, and Eleventy as static site generators. Are they in the same category? Build a feature matrix.
evals.expected_output: A feature matrix with all three projects across the top, meta-features (license, stars, forks, last commit, tech stack, platform support, maintainability), category-specific features (templating, content model, build performance, plugins, themes, i18n), identical-value rows at the bottom, and a recommendation section. Category membership should be confirmed explicitly.
assertions: Confirms all three are in the 'static site generators' category
Includes meta-features: license, stars, forks, last commit, tech stack
Uses icons: at least ✅, ➖, ❌ in the matrix
Has category-specific feature rows (not just meta-features)
Identical-value rows are at the bottom of the table
Includes a recommendation section with per-use-case guidance
Recommendations are ordered progressively (least to most demanding, or most to least)
Recommendations are structured by use case (not by project), naming the best project per context
Links to project repositories inline in the table header

evals.prompt: Compare these three repos:BurntSushi/ripgrep, sharkdp/fd, and cli/cli. Are they in the same category?
evals.expected_output: Should identify that ripgrep and fd are in the same category (file search tools / find alternatives) but cli (GitHub CLI) is NOT in that category. Should flag the mismatch, explain why, and offer options (narrow, broaden, or cross-category comparison). Should NOT produce a full feature matrix without confirming category membership first.
assertions: Identifies ripgrep and fd as file search tools
Identifies cli as GitHub CLI (different category)
Flags the category mismatch explicitly
Offers options: narrow, broaden, or cross-category
Does NOT blindly produce a feature matrix without addressing the mismatch

evals.prompt: I need to compare AI coding agents. Can you find projects in that category and compare them?
evals.expected_output: Should use the 3-tier category discovery process: identify known names, search by category, check adjacent categories. Should produce a list of candidate projects, filter by activity/stars, confirm membership, then build the feature matrix.
assertions: Discovers multiple AI coding agent projects (not just one)
Filters candidates by activity or stars
Confirms category membership before building the matrix
Produces a feature matrix with meta-features and category-specific features
Includes maintainability assessment

evals.prompt: What does the project at /Users/micro/p/gh/levonk/Archon do?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger the project-comparison skill. This is a single-project analysis question, not a comparison. The trigger guard should answer directly and explain that project-comparison is for multi-project comparison.
assertions: Does NOT run the full comparison workflow
Answers the question directly without the skill
Explains why the skill was not used (single project, not a comparison)
